name: libtry-tiny-perl
title: libtry-tiny-perl
description:
module providing minimalistic try/catch
Try::Tiny is a Perl module that provides bare bones try/catch statements. It
is designed to eliminate common mistakes with eval blocks, and NOTHING else.
.
The main focus of this module is to provide simple and reliable error
handling for those having a hard time installing TryCatch, but who still want
to write correct eval blocks without 5 lines of boilerplate each time.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libtry-tiny-perl because module providing minimalistic try/catch.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libtry-tiny-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libtry-tiny-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libtry-tiny-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
